headline: Petrochemical Hub in Pengerang Operates at 52% Capacity
context: The RM72 billion Pengerang Integrated Complex in Johor operated at only 52% capacity in 2025, four years after commissioning, due to technical issues, demand uncertainty, and the 2020 refinery fire that caused RM2.8 billion in damage.

cards.big: RM72 billion Pengerang complex at 52% capacity — Malaysia's largest industrial investment is running at half-speed
cards.sub: Petronas and Saudi Aramco joint venture was meant to transform Johor — 4 years later, it underdelivers on every metric

cards.big: At 52% utilisation, Pengerang's return on investment is estimated at 2.8% — below the 8% minimum for Petronas project viability
cards.sub: Global petrochemical overcapacity from Chinese mega-refineries means Pengerang faces structural demand challenge for years

cards.big: Promised 14,000 permanent jobs materialised as only 6,200 — with 4,800 being contract positions without benefits
cards.sub: 4 nearby fishing villages were relocated for the project — families now live in flats away from their traditional livelihoods

cards.big: 2020 refinery fire investigation found 8 safety violations that went unreported for 14 months before the explosion
cards.sub: DOSH inspection frequency at once per 18 months for a facility this complex is far below international standards

cards.big: RM72 billion committed to a complex running at half-capacity shows what happens when industrial policy follows political ambition, not market reality
Should Petronas publish annual Pengerang performance reviews with independent assessment of whether full-capacity operations are achievable?
